This one's orthodox as well, judging by her speech and technique.
An orthodox potion maker, huh? Let's hear what she has to tell us.
You've... You've come to help break the curse on the great mage?!
Oh, that's wonderful... I thought everyone had forgotten about her after all these eons...
Hey, uh, you're crying.
Forgive me... I was just so moved I couldn't hold the tears back.
For the longest time, I've tried everything to break that curse.
And after all that, I've not made any progress to speak of...
Ah, I'd nearly forgotten! I made this potion just a minute ago.
You there, the commander. Won't you test it for me? I think it's going to work this time!
What? "Test" what?
I'm not cursed, so it won't work on me.
No, no, it's not for breaking curses. This potion should imbue you with vigor.
The inspiration for it hit me just the other day.
Besides, I am but a potion maker. Neutralising a curse is beyond my abilities.
The curse on the great mage affects her heart, dampening her spirit. So I got an idea – why not cancel it out with a potion to cheer her up?
Ohh! Beating the curse at its own game! That's a good idea!
...It's worth a shot, big brother. Try the potion!
Yeah. What's the worst that could happen, right?
Are you serious?
You'll be fine. If the potion has any side effects, I'll cast a spell to bring you back to normal.
Exactly! And if purifying magic doesn't work, I know healing spells that definitely will. You've got nothing to worry about!
You heard them! What harm is a little potion gonna do when you have two clerics by your side?

options.content: This is peer pressure, but fine! I'll drink it!
I want the damn option to say no!

The Village Potion Maker.say: Go on! Drink it!
Bracing myself for the worst, I take the potion from Nubian and chug it all.
"Notification: You have obtained the skill Poison Resistance. Rank: B."
Whoa... It gave you a new skill. I really am a genius potion maker!
Are you? "Poison resistance" doesn't sound like what you had in mind.
That is true... Another failure, it seems.
I'm out of options! I have to move on to the last solution!
And what's that?
To go to the northern tower on Silverbank Island and ask for help from my master – the wise Arkhangelsk!
She is a famous witch who has been around since the age of mythology AND she's the favourite disciple of the ancient goddess of wisdom!
Whoa! You're the disciple of a disciple of that goddess!
You should've said you had such a cool backstory!
There's no time to waste! Let's go see your master right now!
Well... it's not quite that easy.
She has locked herself in that tower and won't speak to anyone but me.
Also, if I leave the village, there'll be no one left to care for the sick.
Ahh... Yeah, they probably wouldn't like it if we took you away like that.
That's it? Pfft! There's an easy fix!
The villagers don't strictly need you to be around, they just need the cures for their ailments.
All you gotta do is create enough all-purpose healing potions for them and they won't mind if you leave the village for a while.
Oh yeah, that would work! You're a genius!
Heehee. I'm just a seasoned adventurer, that's all.
There's still one problem, though...
Here we go...
As I just said, I can't leave the village for long.
I'll need ingredients to make all those potions. Could you get them for me?
Urgh...
Oh.
Ahh...
I knew it. There's no escaping the fetch quests.

The Witch in the Tower.say: To the west of the Royal Capital, in the Looking-Glass Lake, lies Silverbank Island.
We finally got the potion maker to accompany us there... after painstakingly gathering the ingredients to make 99 potions.
After sailing a small boat across the lake, we laid anchor and at last set foot on the island.
The place looks barren, seemingly only home to a dense forest and craggy rocks.
So... where is the northern tower? There's no silver anywhere on this island, just a bunch of trees and bushes.
Are you sure we didn't end up on the wrong island?
I'm sure. This is the right place.
You can't see the tower because my master has hidden it using magic.
She's the kind of lady who likes silence and privacy, you see.
And yes, the island's name may seem deceptive. My master said that this used to be a vast ice lake, long ago. Perhaps that's where the name came from.
Anyway, let's move! Watch my back!
With a smug expression, Nubian walks up to a clearing. She bows to a dead tree and then clears her throat.
My master, the wise witch! It's me, Nubian! I've come to visit!
Then, the space ahead of us seems to warp and bend...
Out of thin air, a tall tower made of black metal manifests.
It's glowing red... H-hang on, isn't this one of the Demon King's bases?
My teacher just likes black and red. Relax, it's got nothing to do with the Demon King!
Follow me. I'll show you inside.
We reach the highest floor of the tower. The legendary witch sits in a chair at the center of a magic circle.
I know why you're here. Leave.
But we haven't said anything yet!
Don't be dumb. She knows EVERYTHING. She's the witch of wisdom, if you forgot.
Master, why won't you help us? We're talking about the great mage who defeated the Demon King a millennium ago.
I believe we should lift the curse on her!
*sigh*... You've always been so naive.
Here's what's going to happen. There is only one person amongst you who can be of use to me.
That is you, Commander. I shall teach you how to break the curse if you stay here and become my disciple.
Hmm... Studying under an extremely wise witch doesn't sound so bad.
Right? You can learn so much about magic and so much more. I can even show you the secrets of the world.
Wait, Commander! Do you realise what becoming her disciple means?
You'll be confined to this tower for ages!
Only after a hundred years will you be allowed to leave, and that's if you pass her beginner's test!
A... A hundred years?
Come on, you're exaggerating... right?
She's not. Training under my watch is done in increments of a hundred years. Don't you know that?
No... Big brother can't leave us for that long!
Yeah! We have a Demon King to defeat!
Uh-huh. I suppose forcing you won't work. I'll give you a chance to prove your worth instead.
I shall teach you how to lift the curse... if you can complete my trial.
A trial from the wise witch, eh. I'm guessing... a pop quiz?
That's what I'd usually do, yes.
However, your Commander possesses a godlike Info Analysis skill. It would make even my hardest quiz trivial.
So, instead, I will have you partake in a combat trial.
Are you ready? Then let's begin!
KABOOOM!
It's over for you!
Herooo Slaaash!
Heheh. That was fantastic. You pass.
Heck yeah! I mean... Of course! Didn't even break a sweat!
Phew... We did it. That was so much work, keeping everyone healed.
Yeah... Thank goodness we could neutralise all those curses.
(Interesting. Those weren't monsters she sicced on us, they were Sirens...)
(She even cast advanced curses on us during the battle.)
Hey, what was up with all those curses and monsters you used?
What about them? I am the all-knowing witch. Of course I can cast advanced curses and control monsters from beyond this world.
Do you want to learn how? Then you should reconsider becoming my disciple.
Master, please give that a rest already!
Poor Nubian. You've turned soft for people ever since you left the tower.
Regardless, a promise is a promise. You passed my trial, so I'll now show you how to lift that curse.
Arkhangelsk holds her hand in the air and an elegant flute appears in her palm.
This is the Flute of Time. A keepsake from the legendary party that great mage was in.
Play this to her and the curse will be lifted.
But after that, you... Never mind, it's not important.
Take the flute and resume your quest, heroes.
Yippee! We got a key item!
(Arkhangelsk was about to say something, but stopped herself. Something tells me it WAS important.)
Don't just stand there, Commander. It's time to leave!
Let's go back to the Endless Woods and lift that stupid curse!

Legendary Heroes.say: Deep in the Endless Woods - Great Mage's Napping Place
Zzz... Mmgh... Zzzzz...
Zzzzz...
She's fallen asleep again.
Given how long she's been cursed, she's probably having a nightmare right now...
Not for long! I'm gonna break your curse, Laffey!
The Flute of Time plays a beautiful sound, causing a dark matter to evaporate from Laffey's body.
The strands of matter combine in the air and swell like a balloon. Then the mass explodes, blanketing the clearing instantly.
I THINK that lifted the curse, but what's happening now?!
...Big brother! Something bad is coming!
I'll put up a barrier just to be safe!
Dammit! The power of the curse is manifesting itself as several monsters!
Be on your guard! We're about to face things that have literally lived for a thousand years!
One by one, the dark presence materializes into human-looking monsters. Moments later, we are deeply surrounded.
What the heck?! These monsters are suppressing my sword's power!
Maybe it's not as strong or legendary as you thought...
I think Bristol was right. She said she'd seen over a hundred like it in a week.
That can't be true! Even if it is, I don't wanna hear it now!
I need a hero's pluck now more than ever!
You know, it's not very herolike to doubt yourself and cower before a battle.
A true hero faces even the mightiest of enemies with courage!
Yes. You have nothing to be afraid of, heroes of the new era. We will help you.
Legendary Heroes.actorName: Pulse of the New Year [Dynamic Kick!]

Legendary Heroes.say: Thank you for lifting the curse on Laffey. Let's deal with these monsters together!
*yawn*... I cast the ultimate explosion spell – Explosion.
Ultimate Ability: Dynamic Kick!
Legendary Heroes.actorName: Pulse of the New Year [Dynamic Kick!]

Legendary Heroes.say: 300% charged! I use Mountain-Splitting Cleave!
Hero's blade, show your true power! Endless Slash!
With the help of the legendary party, the battle was over in a flash.
Sheesh! I knew you were strong, but THIS strong?!
That we are, yes. Keep the praise coming.
Legendary Heroes.actorName: Pulse of the New Year [Dynamic Kick!]

Legendary Heroes.say: Hey, uh, aren't you kind of... fading away?
Fading away?
Ah, you mean this. We're merely phantoms from the past, brought back temporarily by the Flute of Time.
Soon, we'll be gone altogether since we've used up all our strength.
Huh? But Laffey is fading away, too!
Mhm, that's because she's also a phantom.
Planet Bunnyians don't live for thousands of years.
She's only stuck around for so long because of the Demon King's curse, I think.
Now that her curse has been lifted, she's gonna go to heaven with the rest of us.
I can't wait to have sweet dreams again.
Thank you, all of you, for lifting the curse.
But... But you're finally free of the curse, and now you're just... *sob*...
(This must be what Arkhangelsk didn't tell us. She knew this was going to happen.)
Don't cry. I'll give you something as thanks for all you've done...
There. I fully refilled the Flute of Time's mana.
If you're ever in a tight spot, use the flute again to summon us.
This isn't our last meeting. Promise.
Okay...
Oh, new hero! One thing before I go.
It was pretty weird how the monsters suppressed your sword's power. Do you know why?
Because my "hero's" sword is fake?
Yup! It's just an imitation. THIS is the real hero's sword!
Are you serious?! That sword just looks like a big fish!
It sure does!
I'll bet people born after us all thought it looked way too goofy to be a legendary weapon.
That's probably why they made imitations of it look more... normal, like yours.
Oh, but your sword is still a good sword, even if it's not legendary.
But it's only a fake... It's no hero's sword...
Don't think of it like that!
It's not the sword that makes the hero, it's the hero that makes the sword!
Yours will become the new hero's sword if you slay the Demon King with it!
You're right... Yeah, you're totally right!
Thank you, legendary hero Javelin!
Heehee! Glad I could help!
Farewell, new heroes. We will meet again.
Legendary Heroes.actorName: Pulse of the New Year [Dynamic Kick!]

Legendary Heroes.say: Hey, wait! Let me at least get a picture! Smile for the camera!
Perfect! Okay, see you later, legendary heroes!
A gust of wind sends the legendary party back to the heavens. The forest returns to silence.
What a wild turn of events...
Our plan to recruit the great mage has failed spectacularly.
At least we got a legendary item for our troubles.
That flute is sure to keep us safe on our journey!
I guess so.
This was a pretty emotional quest...
But it was also our only lead. What do we do now?
KABOOOM!
Hm? Hey! I hear fighting coming from the south!
Sounds like it's a clash of armies!
Oh no... Is the Demon King Army attacking?!
That can't be! The great wall should keep them out!
Let's go see what's happening!

They Came From the Sky.say: A violent battle unfolds on the road toward Sentinel Bastion.
CHIIIRP!
Out of the way!
Your Holiness, look out!
Freezing Chains, bind them!
Thank you for the assistance!
Your Holiness, we are lightly armed and our foes are many. We will not last in a protracted battle!
I'm aware. Still, we cannot abandon these villagers.
I shall draw the monsters' attention. You two protect the villagers and retreat!
No! You can't, Your Holiness!
It is the only way. Please do as I say!
Meanwhile, across the battlefield...
Those Dark Slimejuu Knights ordered the attack...
This is bad. The Demon King Army is attacking AND it's their airborne hunters division!
Who's that fighting over there? The Royal Army?
Yes, that's– Wait, is that the Saint herself?!
What's the Saint doing this far from the Royal Capital?!
Big brother, this is serious!
The Royal Army probably won't hold out for long!
Everyone, get ready for battle! We're going to help them!
KABOOOM!
Phew... That was the last one.
Thank you for coming to our aid, adventurers.
We would have suffered major casualties were it not for you.
Don't mention it! Helping those in need is just what heroes do!
Your Holiness, why'd you fight the Demon King Army with such a small force?!
Well... I didn't want to fight any monsters today.
I simply saw these villagers being attacked whilst en route to Sentinel Bastion, and I helped them.
It's very alarming that the Demon King Army is now attacking settlements even this close to the Bastion...
I fear that the situation on the frontlines is getting worse.
Adventurers, may I beseech you something?
Sure! What do you need?
Our great wall is protected by many goddesses, including the goddess of light and justice. Even the Demon King cannot break through it so easily.
However, her army tore a hole through it during a major battle a few days ago.
I left the Royal Capital specifically to help repair the hole. Alas, I did not anticipate the Demon King Army would attack a settlement so close to it.
I believe the Demon King means to exploit this gap in our defenses to make a move.
So, I want to travel to the Bastion and reinforce it, but I cannot just leave these poor villagers to fend for themselves.
They are all evacuees from Friede, a village that sits close to the hole.
It's safe to assume that monsters are laying waste to their homes as we speak.
Which brings me to my request – would you please clear the village of monsters?
Adventurers! Please save our village!
Killing monsters and saving a village? Sounds exactly like a quest fit for a hero!

options.content: Only if the reward is right.

They Came From the Sky.say: What do you say, Commander?
There you have it! The Commander said so!
Uh, of course... We'll give you a heap of rewards if you do it!
That is, if you survive...
Alright, the hero's party is on the case!
Thank you! May the goddess of peace and protection be with you!

To Friede Village's Rescue.say: Friede Village – it gets its name from the word Frieden, which means "peace."
A rather ironic name since it is now engulfed in the flames of war.
Half the buildings have been burned to the ground...
Weird. There isn't a villager or monster in sight.
Halt! You there! Identify yourselves!
You look like bandits raring to loot the ruins! Not on my watch!
Who are YOU calling a bandit?!
I am the hero, and this is my party! We're on a journey to defeat the Demon King! Now who are you?
Ah... Adventurers.
Apologies for suspecting you.
I am Vanguard, knight banneret and guardswoman from the west wall.
I came after receiving word that Friede was under attack.
Don't worry. We've already eliminated every last monster.
That's what you came here to do, I presume.
Yes, and... now you've kind of left us with nothing to do.
No rewards for this side quest, I guess.
You know what that means, Commander. Back to the main quest.
Hang on. This quest isn't finished yet.
Huh? It's not?
Vanguard, you said you're a guardswoman from the west wall.
I've heard that a battle just a while ago tore a hole through it. Is that right?
That's strictly confidential information. Who told you that?
Doesn't matter. What I wanted to ask is – was it really a good idea to take your troops and leave the west wall unguarded?
Who's defending the hole now?
If it's nobody, the Demon King Army is probably going to target it.
Urgh! You've got a good point...
I need to get back to the wall right away!
KABOOOM!
Sounds like another battle!
This time it's coming from over by the wall!
Son of a...
Soldiers, regroup! Return to the wall immediately!
We'll back you up!
Many thanks!

Attack on the Wall.say: Swarms of flying monsters hang in the sky like dark clouds. They descend upon the wall with the force of a squall.
Swords and magic and machinery and armor clashed and collided with one another.
By the time we arrive, there is already a heated battle unfolding in the air above the wall.
They've sent in flying monsters AND an undead battalion... They're going all-out.
This is going to be a fierce battle...
Let them come! We are knights of the Royal Army! We shall not falter!
Hold the line! Let not a single monster through!
Behold the light of my royal sabre!
Hey! Sorry I'm late!
What a superb attack!
You came not a moment too soon, Vanguard.
Had you taken any longer, there would've been no monsters left for you.
So... How's the village?
We cleared it of monsters. All the villagers got out safely.
That's great news.
Still, I was careless to fall for the Demon King's diversion. I should've known better.
Known better and done what, exactly?
The wall's entire raison d'etre is to protect our people against the Demon King Army.
Coming to their aid is nothing to be ashamed of. Leaving them to fend for themselves – that would be.
Yes... You're right.
At any rate, that problem has been dealt with. Now it's time to deal with this one.
Tell me, what are we up against?
Their army is attacking on all fronts. It makes our battle a few days ago look like a tavern fight.
Their forces are led by a general of their army – Dragonlord Blücher.
The Dragonlord? Heh, I like a challenge.
Heed my words, knight of darkness. You will suffer the pain that you've inflicted upon our people twofold!
Mwahahahaha☆
Buuurn! Turn to ashes!
Ahah! Their positions are in tatters! Milord has proved once again what a brilliant strategist she is!
Crushing these humans is gonna be child's–
Hey! You! Are you the Dragonlord?
That title is bigger than you are!
I know what you're doing! You're trying to hide how small your force actually is!
I'm gonna knock you off that dragon and bring you down to earth!
Oh? An adventurer?
Mwaha☆ They're so desperate, they're relying on adventurers to defend their wall now!
Hmph! Since you clearly don't understand the disadvantage you're at, I'll show you!
Did you hear that? Blücher took the bait!
Hah! This is gonna be such an easy win!
Indeed. Even the strongest army will lose if its general is compromised.
Now we wait for our chance to shoot her down.
Roger! I've been waiting for an excuse to draw my sword!
O goddess of light and justice, give us thy blessed light and let our brave warriors be victorious...
O goddess of steel and dragons, unleash thy fury upon our foes!
What the... The Holy Church and the Church of Steel are here!
Everyone! Hold firm in your belief of victory!
Reinforcements have arrived! Let us smite these monsters together!
KABOOOM!
Ugh! Seriously?!
Don't assume you've won already!
Revenge WILL be mine!
I'll be back, and then– Whoa, whooooaaaa! Mind the turbulence, dragooooon!
Blücher is dragged along helplessly by her dragon. The two disappear like shooting stars among the distant dark clouds.
Dang. The dragon's scales were too thick and it got away.
Perhaps so, but this is still a major victory for us.
With their general out of the picture, it's only a matter of time before their rank and file collapses.
And to think you did this by provoking her, then taking her out with flawless teamwork...
You truly are an exceptional strategist, Commander.

options.content: We will, and that's a promise!

Attack on the Wall.say: One day, you and your party may just defeat the Demon King and restore peace to the world.
Heehee. Your confidence brings me hope.
I look forward to the day I'll hear about how your group took the Demon King out.
It's not like we've defeated their whole army yet. Save the praise and medals for when it's warranted.
Yes, I suppose. We mustn't let our guard down.
Then let us speak again come the victory celebration.

All in a Day's Work.say: After the battle ended, we were formally invited to partake in the victory celebration at Sentinel Bastion.
This place serves as the center of operations for the wall's defenders. Yet, once upon a time, it was no more than an unremarkable little village.
That changed when the Demon King Army assaulted the walls of the old castle, decimating it.
Following its destruction, the heads of the last royal family of the old dynasty beseeched the goddesses for help. With their aid, this great wall was built, dividing the land in two.
It was thanks to this wall that the kingdom of humanity survived, narrowly escaping ruination at the hands of the Demon King Army.
Then, the Royal Capital was rebuilt, and the kingdom managed to prosper once more.
Little by little, this place – the beating heart of the great wall – became a second capital, growing into what is now known as Sentinel Bastion.
This brings us to today, where the streets are littered with colorful decor and the people cheer loudly.
To celebrate humanity's victory in an important battle.
Sentinel Bastion - Banquet Hall
Incredible. You actually managed to lift the curse on the great mage...
And not only that, you even spoke to the old heroes! I almost can't believe it.
You should! I haven't even told you the most shocking detail yet...
You know the legendary hero's sword? All this time, it was actually shaped like a fish!
Shaped like a fish? Come on, nobody's gonna believe that! You're just making it up!
Am not! You think I would lie about something so lame?
Alright, settle down. Whether it's the truth or not doesn't really matter.
Now lookie here and pose for the camera!
Bleh... What the heck is this jiggly stuff?
It's my favourite and speciality – eel jelly! I think it's the perfect for a victory banquet.
Though, full disclosure: I couldn't find any eels, so I used Eel Slimejuu meat instead. It's pretty similar in texture!
Come on, don't be shy. Try it!
Slimejuu meat? Are you serious? You just stuffed a monster into your food!
Th-thanks, kind adventurer, but I'm not hungry. I'll have to pass on this one.
I'm sure your cooking would be lovely after a drink or–
Did somebody say drink? I've got just the thing for you!
...Huh?
Excuse me, what's this? It's pitch black, yet it's somehow glowing...
It's a very special drink, made by yours truly.
Drinking it will make all your fatigue from fighting go away, just like that!
I somehow don't believe that! Besides, a knight like me doesn't tire after one mere day of combat!
I appreciate the thought, but please find someone else to give your drink.
Come ooon, I made it just for the banquet! At least have a small sip! Please?
NO! I will NOT drink it!
A fun and friendly atmosphere hangs in the banquet air.
Prim and proper knights mingle and chat with laid-back adventurers, all having a good time.
And then there's me and the thought in the back of my mind that prevents me from really enjoying myself.
That general of the undead army dropped this golden coin after we defeated her...
My Info Analysis skill says this coin was produced back in the age of the old kingdom.
Why did a general of the Demon King Army have a coin like this?
It almost makes me think the old kingdom, the undead army, and even the Demon King are all somehow connected...
But in what way, I have no idea. Too little to work with.
This lore stuff is pretty fascinating. Really tickles your imagination.
Masteeer!
Commandeeer!
It seems if I can't find fun at the banquet, the fun will find me.
*sob*... Nobody wants to eat the food I made!
*sob*... And nobody wants to drink my special potion either!
Please, pleeease eat it!
Please, pleeease drink it!
(I DO have an SSS-rank Curse Resistance. My gut should be able to handle this.)
Of course, uh...
Urgh... What happened?
I must've passed out...
I feel something warm and soft against my head.
Something smells nice, too...
Oh... You're awake.
I scolded Liverpool and Nubian for what they did.
It's like as soon as I'm not here, they start feeding you weird stuff...
Did you look after me while I was out cold? Thanks for that.
Heehee... How do you feel now?
Fine. I'm right as rain.
Turns out that Poison Resistance really came in clutch.
You won't kill the Demon King anytime soon if you're getting knocked out even at a party.
Yeah. Imagine if I died not in a battle, but at a banquet attended by healers, angels, and clerics...
I'd be a laughing stock for the rest of time.
I sense subtle mockery in your words...
At any rate, it's good that you're safe. I have something important to discuss, so I'll go and gather your party members.
Sometime later, in Sentinel Bastion's assembly room...
...In light of these achievements, Her Majesty has decided not to hold you accountable for the destruction of her audience hall.
Additionally, your party shall be granted permits to freely pass through Sentinel Bastion.
Naturally, you will need equipment and supplies to brave the Wasteland. The Royal Army shall provide you with those.
Later, you may also visit our armory and pick any weapons you'd like.
Wait, what was that about permits?
Do you need a PERMIT to go off and kill the Demon King?
Of course you do.
The Wasteland is dangerous, and our kingdom needs every warrior it can get. We cannot simply let people wander toward pointless deaths.
Oh... Good thing we decided to help defend the wall.
Our journey to slay the Demon King would've been over without those permits.
You would also have been seized by the guards and brought back to the castle to answer for your crimes.
Ahahahaha...
Ahem... On behalf of Her Majesty, I would like to thank you all for your bravery in that battle.
Furthermore, I, personally, have a special reward to give you, Commander. For your outstanding contributions.
A special reward? What is it?
Joffre, go ahead.
Yes, ma'am.
Commander, given your numerous successes already, it has been determined that you have the latent ability to lead the party that shall slay the Demon King.
In the name of the goddess of light and justice, I hereby grant you the Blessing of Light.
I hope that it will help you on your way to the Demon King.
Joffre the Fighting Angel offers a prayer, and a golden light illuminates the surroundings.
"Notification: You have obtained the skill Blessing of Light. Rank: SSS."
Now you carry the blessing with you.
Thus, the ceremony has concluded, and my work for the night is done.
You may return to the banquet hall and enjoy the rest of your evening.
I hope you have a pleasant time.
Sentinel Bastion - Guest Room
It's late in the night, and a woman stands alone in a pitch-black room.
She awarded that blessing...
That's mean of you, Saint. Stealing the honor like that.
But while the Blessing of Light protects your mind, it won't be enough for the party to defeat the Demon King.
Doing that will require my Blessing of Steel, which protects the flesh.
...Oh, seems my spell didn't completely put you to sleep, little one.
Heehee, though it doesn't look like you'll be fully waking up soon either.
Until then, I want you to take my love.
You should feel stronger once you wake up.
And this – just think of it like a sweet dream on your quest to kill the Demon King.
Nighty night, my dear little one.

The Girls of the Withered Woods.say: Following the battle, our party stayed at Sentinel Bastion for three days to rest and recover.
We upgraded our gear and stocked up on supplies, then read up on the Demon King Army as much as we could.
Then, finally, we passed through the main gates, officially taking our first step into the monster-occupied Wasteland.
We followed the established road leading away from Sentinel Bastion, but it wasn't long before it abruptly stopped.
We kept going in the same direction and eventually ended up in a thick forest stuffy with fog.
The trees all around us look twisted and mad, their desiccated branches reaching for the sky like broken arms.
Big brother, this place is creepy...
I think we've entered what my skills call the Withered Woods.
Correct. These are the Withered Woods.
A figure appears from the mist – it's Jeanne d'Arc, the saint of the Holy Church.
I've been waiting for you.
I wish to join you. I believe my assistance will bring about the Demon King's defeat just a bit sooner.
That is, if you'll let me. Will you?
Of course. Welcome to the team.
Heehee. Thank you. You won't regret this.
Ah, so you've already deduced why I'm here.
Indeed, I've been waiting for you here so I could join you on your quest.
I believe that coming with you should bring about the Demon King's defeat sooner, even if only slightly.
As I was about to say... This is the Wasteland, dubbed so for obvious reasons.
This dramatic change is the result of a curse that was placed on it. A spell simply known as the Demon King's Curse.
Long ago, before even the great wall was built, these woods were actually an extension of the Endless Woods.
But now, this place has been reduced to a restricted area, its fauna withering and lava streams raging.
To the best of our knowledge, the Demon King and her army have established three bases on this side of the wall.
These are the fortresses to the west, east, and center of the Wasteland.
The Demon King Army has been applying pressure on our great wall through these bases.
We have embarked on holy expeditions before in attempts to purify the Wasteland... but all were unsuccessful.
Even if you purify one area, there are still the three bases that propagate the curse.
You can bless the purified land to keep it pristine for a while, but once the blessing wears off, the curse will reclaim it.
So it's not that purification doesn't work, it's that those bases undo its effects.
If we can neutralize the bases, the land won't be ruined again, right? Even without blessing it.
Indeed, but it should be noted that you CAN bless the land, Commander.
You carry the blessing of the goddess of light and justice in your heart. It can purify the land if you just destroy those bases.
What? The Commander can do that?!
What about ME?
When will it be MY time to shine?
Umm... I suppose your glorious duty is to slay the monsters and capture the bases.
...Good point!
You're right! It IS my duty to slay the monsters!
So, what are we waiting for? Let's find a base and clear it out!
Shhh! Quiet!
My master told me this place is inhabited by ferocious monsters that–
CHIRP CHIRP CHIRP!
Bugger, they know we're here...
Oh, fiddlesticks!
Change formation! Prepare for battle!
CHIIIRP CHIRP!
CHIRP! CHIRP!
There are too many of them! Too many even for me to handle!
I'm out of MP...
Yeah, same...
Take these MP recovery potions! I've got healing potions left, too, so I'll cover you!
Commander, we will be overwhelmed if this continues!
The monsters on this side of the wall are something else entirely...
We should avoid head-on confrontations with them until we know their weaknesses.
At least someone among you has a working brain.
That said, if you were truly smart, you never would've come here to begin with.
I've deployed a smokescreen to distract them. Recover now while you can.
What a party you have...
A hero, two clerics, a potion maker, a commander, and...
Well, if it isn't the Royal Army's saint.
You look familiar... Are you... a vampire hunter?
They say you're a resistance group that fights the Demon King Army from this side of the wall.
I thought the stories about you were just that – stories.
It's better for us if some people believe that.
Well met, Your Holiness. I'm Sheffield, a vampire hunter, as you surmised.
I'm curious, what are you doing out here? Did you leave the safety of your castle to go on an adventure?
I took you for a coward, hiding behind that wall whilst sending adventurers to their deaths in the land you abandoned.
I do not take kindly to insults directed at the soldiers of the Royal Army!
We fight day in and day out to defeat the Demon King!
Furthermore, we support those brave souls who venture into the Wasteland and treat them with the utmost respect!
Defeat the Demon King, you say?
Do you know how much time has passed since your last holy expedition?
Has even a single one been carried out since you were born?
Well... I...
Now this is getting interesting. You snuck out without telling anyone, didn't you, Ms. Saint?
Wait, you didn't tell anyone?!
Sooo... we're suspects in helping her escape now...
If the army captures us, they will DEFINITELY throw us in jail this time!
That...
That won't happen, I assure you. I did this entirely on my own.
Whatever consequences arise from this, I shall bear myself. I will not let them punish you.
I know you won't.

options.content: We'll need all the manpower we can get.
I just thought holding you hostage would be fun.

The Girls of the Withered Woods.say: ...You knew that I snuck out, didn't you, Commander? And let me into your party nevertheless?
That is true. The more people on your side against the Demon King, the better.
Nobody will mind that you snuck out if you and the rest of us defeat the Demon King, either.
I suppose that's– Wait, you thought even that far head?
H-holding me hostage?!
Big brother...
Sorry, sorry. It was just a joke.
Heh. Your party sounds like a fun bunch.
Even if you weren't, the vampire hunters welcome anyone who ventures to this side of the wall.
Now, is everyone topped off on health?
Then come with me if you want to live. I'll bring you to a safe place.

The Vampire Hunters.say: Following Sheffield's lead, we travel to the center of the Withered Woods.
The bases that you want to capture are protected by a strong magic barrier known as "the Neverending Night."
It's a closely-guarded vampire spell that makes it impossible to pass through. It must be dispelled first.
It's due to this barrier that we've been unable to capture any base for so long.
Speaking of the bases, the central one is the most well-protected of the three.
The Demon King has focused most of her forces there as well, owing to its proximity to Sentinel Bastion.
It's tragic, thinking of all the adventurers who've perished to it.
It lies so close to the main gate, and yet it's so out of their league...
Yes... And we got very close to being its latest victims.
You're lucky that I ran into you.
Luck is an important thing to have. Sometimes it can even be more important than anything else.
This luck was the potential I saw within your party, and it's why I decided to save you.
...Keep following me. Our base is just up ahead.
Sheffield looks forward at what looks only to be a pile of desolate ruins.
Your base... is among those ruins?
Not quite. Below.
It's well-hidden, so I don't expect you to see where it is.
I keep scanning the ruins from afar, and eventually, the ruins of a majestic castle wall catch my attention.
While it is crumbled down for the most, some parts of it still stand proudly intact.
Are those the ruins of the old castle wall?
Yes. People like to call it "the Royal Crown Wall Ruins."
Though it may be far before your time, to us... it's like it was still pristine only yesterday.
Sheffy, I see you've dragged in lost adventurers again.
Please stop. I don't want to hear this again.
Heehee, I know, I know. Come on, adventurers, let's talk inside. We don't want a patrol to catch sight of us.
We pass through the ruin entrance guarded by a magic spell and enter the vampire hunters' base.
I expected some sort of dank dungeon, but it's actually quite spacious and comfortable down here.
We've been living here for a long time now. We couldn't just leave this place in a sorry state.
A long time, eh... I can't hold back my curiosity – just how old are you people?
How old?
Hah. Either you haven't done your homework, or your kingdom hardly knows anything about us.
We vampire hunters have existed since before the old castle fell.
Hang on... Didn't it fall several hundred years ago?
That it did. It's almost hard to believe it's been that long.
The vampires now under the Demon King's command weren't always like that. They were once nobles who resided in the kingdom.
And, in fact, we humans and vampires used to coexist long, long ago.
Then came that horrible day, when the Demon King Army marched in...
Whilst the city deployed most of its army at the front, those opportunistic vampire bastards staged a rebellion.
This rebellion led to the city's downfall. From that day on, Ballad Castle came to be known as the Songless Castle.
Time moved on, and one day it became known on the map only as "the Old Royal Capital Ruins."
We are, in every sense, survivors from that calamity that befell the city.
We were commoners, guards, maids, merchants, artisans... All of us had different social standings before the downfall.
Now, we are all united under the title of vampire hunter.
We made a promise. To keep fighting until the day either we or the vampires have been wiped out.
My word... I never knew the truth behind the old city.
This part of history was completely lost amidst the chaos of relocating the city.
What's more, I thought it was only the Demon King who brought down the city through brute force...
Hah. If she had that kind of power, your great wall wouldn't be able to stop her.
...So you were once humans like us.
But it's been centuries, and humans don't live that long. How are you still alive?
You're right, humans don't live for centuries. Not most humans, anyway.
I think that maybe the catastrophe was so horrific that some goddesses took pity on those who survived it.
They gave us their blessings, and that has allowed us to live to this day.
Everyone who isn't here... They met a tragic end.
Those who lost their lives amidst the initial fighting, they were the lucky ones.
Many, many more were turned into undead slaves puppeteered by the vampires after inhaling a cursed mist.
Those traitorous vampires presented their "achievement" to the Demon King and gladly joined her side. Today, they're second-in-command only to the Demon King herself.
(Blessings and curses... Interesting.)
(A blessing from the goddesses extended the vampire hunters' lifespans, while Laffey's was extended with a curse.)
(You could even say those who were turned into undeads were given longer lives in a twisted way.)
(If these phenomena are all linked somehow, then what happened to the old capital may be even more complicated than I thought...)
Do you still remember the exact location of the old capital?
Wait... You're not thinking about exploring the ruins, are you?
Frankly, you should give up while you're ahead.
Those ruins have been the undeads' main base ever since the downfall. Even we can't approach them on a whim.
Sheffield told us about the vampire spell that protects the bases. The Neverending Night.
If we suppose that the old city was the vampires' headquarters and that it fell in a very short span of time...
Then they probably left the key to lifting the spell somewhere among the ruins.
If we find it, we can break the barrier and defeat those vampires that betrayed you so long ago.
You make a good point. Knowing my Master's outstanding luck, finding it might just be possible.
Very well... Then we shall go there.
Not today, though – it's late as it is. Let's rest up, then go treasure-hunting in the ruins tomorrow morning!

The Pirates of Tempesta.say: Ballad Castle – it got its name from the endless sound of music that came from it, never drowned out by the streets.
This was once the heart of the kingdom of humanity. Though now it's but a husk of its former self, one can still see a glimpse of its former glory.
How I've missed you, Ballad Castle.
Ruined though it may be, this place's structure hasn't changed much since it was spared from the worst of the fighting.
The vampire nobles all used to live in the castle. This road should take us to...
...Strange. I don't see a single undead anywhere.
Yes, this is supposed to be their nest... Where have they all gone?
*yaaawn*... Who do you think you are, setting foot on Tempesta's turf?
Uh... "Tempesta's turf"?
Well, well! Seems we have ourselves a band of adventurers with a death wish!
Heehee, what brings you here? Hoping to find buried treasure among the ruins, hmm?
We run this place! You wanna pass through? Then hand over your valuables!
Ohhh. They're just bandits.
Bandits? Slander! We are decent, hardworking pirates!
Pirates on land? I've never seen that before...
Well, now you have! We're pirates, AND we're on la– Wait, I'm getting sidetracked again.
Hey, what were we talking about?
You were saying, "Hand over your valuables."
Oh, right. Valuables, NOW!
A gang of lowly pirates, trying to shake down the hero's party? That's rich!
All you're gonna get from us is a lesson in respect!
No... Little guys, wait.
Look at their commander... Does anyone else recognise that aura?
Oh... Is that the Blessing of Light?
The blessing bestowed by the goddess of light and justice... They're allies of the Royal Army!
Hey! Commander! Let's stop fighting!
This is all a big misunderstanding!
A misunderstanding?
Yeah. You heard her.
It's actually great that you're here. We've been waiting for this day for an eternity.
Anyway, will you pay the rest of our reward?
*sigh*... Right, it's been centuries...
Okay, story time. We used to be free pirates who occasionally committed petty crimes.
Every now and then, our crew made landfall to resupply and catch a break from the sea.
Whenever we did, we'd offer ourselves as mercenaries in exchange for a bit of coin.
One day, we'd completed a really high-paying job and went to the city to celebrate.
We were having a meal when, all of a sudden, chaos broke out on the streets.
Then this bigwig royal showed up and was like, "Won't you come to the castle and be my guards?"
I was gonna say, "You think Tempesta is a bunch of sellouts, you dip?" But that sum we were offered was too big to refuse.
But there was a secret catch – that this protection gig would last hundreds of years!
We were here to witness that curse fall upon the city, turning almost everyone into a mindless undead.
The goddess of pioneering and adventure must've rewarded us for our faith, because we were spared that fate. Sort of. We've existed as ghost pirates ever since then.
Being a ghost pirate IS as cool as it sounds, don't get me wrong...
But being stuck in one place for countless generations suuucks! I'm fed up with it! I wish I could quit!
Now, finally... FINALLY our payday has come.
Geesh... That's a pretty sad story.
Even if it sounds like it's waiting for a punchline...
No matter! A hero should embody the spirit of chivalry!
If it's money you want, they gave us a load of it back at Sentinel Bastion!
Here! This should cover everything!
Fu Shun assumes a cool attitude and produces a bag of gold, dropping it on the floor.
Let's see... What the heck is this? Anyone recognise these?
Can't say I do. I've never seen coins like these.
Bastards! You trying to rip us off with counterfeits?!
Counterfeits?! This is the real stuff they use in the city!
Ahh. These pirates have been stuck here for so long that they don't know the kingdom's currency has changed.
We'll need coins they're familiar with if we want to pass through here.
Speaking of which... it just so happens I have one.
That's more like it. I knew you had the real deal on you!
One single coin isn't gonna cut it, though!
I know it won't. This is just a sign of goodwill.
I hope it's enough to let us pass. We'll pay you the rest once we've finished our business inside the castle.
...Alright. You have a deal.
Little guys, clear the path and let the adventurers through.

Remnants of Bygone Days.say: Old City Ruins - Castle
Commander, do you think we'll really find enough coins amongst these ruins?
I do. Call it a hunch.
They are so committed to their job, they're still waiting for their debt to be paid even after turning into ghosts.
That commitment is what has kept this castle protected for hundreds of years.
The castle which we just so happen to need to get into.
Everything lines up too perfectly to be a coincidence.

options.content: (Use Info Analysis on the ruins of the hall.)

Remnants of Bygone Days.say: That reminds me... Those ghosts have "lived" very long. Maybe that, too, is somehow connected to the vampire hunters.
Look! The gemstone in that wall is glowing!
Why, I'll be... A secret room, perchance?
Some mechanism activates, slowly sliding the thick wall aside and revealing a study that has been hidden for countless years.
An old smell hangs in the air and a carpet on the floor is buried under a layer of dust. The mana-powered lamps are working, though their light has become very dim.
At the other end of the room is a table, atop which a golden sphere of magic silently hovers.
This spell is royal magic. It protects against time-related degradation.
It is ordinarily used to preserve cultural artifacts and important documents, but– Oh!
The magic sphere dispels after being touched, revealing that which it has protected for all these centuries...
A handful of papers and a letter.
I am Duke of York, the matriarch of the vampire family in the human capital.
Or, to be precise, I WAS the matriarch.
I do not know when this letter will be read, or if it will even be found at all.
Regardless, it is my duty to record the events that unfolded here.
A month prior, a strange disease began to spread between my family members.
At first, I thought it was a simple weakening curse.
However, those afflicted with it slowly but surely lost their connection to our family. I realised that they had begun heeding someone else's voice.
For long did I try to learn more, and one day I discovered that the voice was the Demon King's. This "disease" was in fact a plot to control us.
But by the time I knew, it was too late to do anything about it.
Now, as I write this, my beloved city is ablaze because of my kin's betrayal.
I don't have much time left.
Therefore, I have decided to employ the last ace up my sleeve.
If it succeeds, it may save at least some lives...
As well as protect the truth hidden within this chamber.
In the corner, there is a chest with enough gold to pay the debt to the pirates.
On the desk lies two written spells – one to break the Neverending Night, and one to exterminate the vampires.
To whoever finds this letter...
Please do what is right and set my family free so–
The writing becomes increasingly messy before abruptly stopping.
So the vampires didn't betray us, they were simply manipulated by the Demon King...
And the reason we've all survived to this day is because of the vampires' spell...
I can't say I'm happy to know that this is what the truth is like.
But it is the truth, even if it took this long to discover.
My Master is right. However much time has passed, there is no changing the truth.
We now know why the city fell and who our real enemy is.
Let's go, Commander. We've learned all that we'll need.
We must destroy those three strongholds as soon as possible.
After leaving the secret room, we give the whole chest full of old coins to the Tempesta pirates.
Wait... THIS much?
This is way more than what we're owed, even with centuries of interest on top.
Goodness me, adventurers. Your generosity knows no bounds!
Anyone who pays their dues so handsomely is a friend in our eyes♡
As proof of our friendship, take this Seatide Horn. I'm sure it'll come in useful♪
Just toot it whenever you find yourself in trouble and Tempesta will be there in a flash!
"Notification: You have obtained the skill Seatide Horn. Rank: SSS."
"Notification: You have obtained the maximum amount of skills. You need to either forget or replace a skill."
Hmm... Throwing is my only combat skill, so I can't give that one up.
There's my Sprinting skill. I've got absolutely no use out of it so far.
Besides, it's not like it's impossible to sprint without the skill... right?
I press the button for the Sprinting skill, replacing it with Seatide Horn.

The Eastern Fortress.say: Casting the spell written on the paper, we swiftly dispelled the Neverending Night and entered the Eastern Fortress.
The vampires who reside here have no idea that their protective spell has been neutralized. We quickly overwhelm them with little resistance.
*cough cough*... That spell...
You must've gotten it from Miss York... How do you know her?
...Oh, it's all coming to me now.
I can finally think clearly now that the spell has been lifted.
I can't believe how long it's been...
After all these centuries... I think it's time for me to sleep.
What are you going to do? Take down all three fortresses?
Killing me will put the other bases on high alert. You won't catch them off guard again.
Bit of advice: the princess and the head maid are personally guarding the central base. I'd go there last if I were you.
Take care of the western fortress first.
Elbing oversees its defenses. She has a real sweet tooth.
She's a sucker especially for creative desserts.
I'll bet it's been ages since she's had any because of the Demon King Army's scorched earth approach.
Disguise yourselves as smugglers passing through the area and tell her you'll give her a delicious dessert.
If you're lucky, she might just come to see you in person.
That'll be your chance to strike.
...It's time for me to move on.
Good luck, adventurers. Kill the Demon King.

The Western Fortress.say: Withered Woods - Road to the Western Fortress
This next vampire loves creative desserts... How are we going to make something like that?
Desserts are all about the taste.
I think we just need something that's different from run-of-the-mill stuff.
It just so happens I have a bunch of potions that can change how something tastes!
It should use ingredients not found in ordinary desserts, too!
Slimejuu flesh would be just the thing! That's GOT to be creative!
Don't forget the color and shape!
Innovating in those areas is just as important!
Let's make it, I dunno... colorful! Splash it with the whole rainbow!
As for the shape, it's got to be eye-catching! Something that gets your attention immediately!

options.content: The smell! Don't forget the smell!

The Western Fortress.say: So, what I propose is – a big and colorful time bomb Slimejuu cake!
Taste is the combination of flavor, smell, and appearance. A creative dessert must smell novel, too.
It should be unlike anything she has ever smelled before!
Of course! Leave it to the Commander to patch up any holes in our plans!
A novel smell... I'll see if I have a potion that fits the bill!
B-big brother... Are you sure this will produce something edible?
Maybe not to us, but who knows? Maybe vampires have different tastes from humans?
B-big brother... Are you sure this will produce something edible?
Let's just pray that vampires have different tastes from humans...
This is... interesting, I'll say that.
I'll have to sit this one out, I'm afraid. I'm a rather poor actor.
Instead, I'll observe the enemy's movements at the stronghold. Good luck, Master.
Our party approaches the eastern fortress, finished Slimejuu cake held high for all to see.
Just as we'd hoped, we had encountered not a single monster along the way.
Now that is a very creative cake you have...
Say, are you smugglers passing through my territory?
Very well. Leave the cake and pass through.
Wait, actually... Hold on to the cake for me. I want to taste it at once!
Heh! She bought it! The cake has distracted her!
This cake was a real doozy to make, but at least it's serving its purpose.
Careless and clueless, Elbing walks up to Fu Shun. Her attention is entirely on the cake.
Then... Whoosh! The vampire hunters leap out of hiding and score a hit that frees her from the Demon King's spell.
Ahh... My life is slipping from me...
But... the curse's grip is lifting...
I'm free. Free at long last...
I've never seen a cake like yours before. You've shown me a glimpse of true creativity.
And for that, I thank you...
Please, save my sister and the head maid as well...
May fortune be with you, O righteous adventurers...

The Central Fortress.say: In the Withered Woods, while we're on our way to the central fortress...
...Things have changed. We need to come up with a new strategy at once.
Sheffy? What's the matter?
The central fortress is doing battle with the Demon King Army.
What? They're fighting each other?
You heard me.
Since their other two fortresses have fallen, perhaps the Demon King is in a rush to install a new leader.
It doesn't seem the takeover process is going too well. At any rate, the two sides are now duking it out.
Sounds like a golden opportunity! Let's go check it out, Commander!
By the time we reach the inside of the fortress, the fighting has already concluded.
Ugghh... This is NOT how I thought I'd go...
It's okay, Lützow. I'm going with you.
What?! They've already taken out two of the vampires?!
Heheheh. What have we here? Passing adventurers?
Perfect timing. I feared there'd be not a soul around to gaze upon my splendid visage.
Who are you?!
Me?
I am Vampire, the most flawless and powerful vampire of all!
As milord hath instructed me, I have taken over this fortress.
Those last-generation vampires were so weak, yet so stubborn. All I did was send them to heaven.
Master, she's right about one thing – she is very powerful.
On the other hand, she has been injured, that much is clear.
Yes, I noticed that as well.
Beware, though – she recovers astoundingly quickly. If we're going to attack, now is the time.
My adventure interface provides a description of her, confirming that she is indeed a mighty vampire with no weaknesses.
On the bright side, she is only at half HP.
In addition, my skill tells me that there is a secret trap waiting to be sprung inside the room.
(It's hidden within a folded space. That vampire maid must've rigged it before she passed on.)
(Let's see if we can use it to our advantage in this battle.)
Everyone, into positions! Get ready for battle!
Hahah! Thou art challenging me? Suicidal fools!
Thou shall witness the terrifying might of the ultimate vampire!
KABOOOM!
Hah! Is that all? Thou think this will hold back the unsurpassable vampire?
I summon thee, my minions! Give these impudent humans sweet, eternal release!
CHIRP CHIIIRP!
Unicorn, Liverpool! Keep your MP consumption under control and try to stick to healing over time spells with minimal mana cost!
Nubian, throw every last explosive potion you have at that flock of monsters!
Sheffield, Seydlitz, focus on eliminating isolated monsters from afar, one at a time!
Jeanne d'Arc, stay within the healing circle and deal with any monsters that come close!
Fu Shun, get back in formation! You're overextending!
Heh. Not giving me any openings, are thee?
I shall deal with thou first, leader of this party!
She's finally taken the bait.
Yelling my orders for all to hear was the winning move.
The vampire comes charging straight toward me... and springs the trap that the maid laid before she left this world.

options.content: I knew that trap was going to be good!

The Central Fortress.say: Then everything stops. Time is frozen.
Toki yo, tomare!
My... body... I can't move!
Say goodnight, vampire!
Damn you, humaaans!
Now, all three of the Demon King's bases have been destroyed.
My Blessing of Light repels the wretched curse, restoring life to the Wasteland.
When I first saw you, I thought you were just another bunch of reckless adventurers.
I've never been happier to be proven completely wrong.
On behalf of all vampire hunters, thank you for all you've done.
If you ever need assistance, we will always answer your call.
Now that our battle here is over, we're going to spread out and tell other hunters the truth of what happened to the city all those years ago.
As such, I doubt we will see each other for a short while.
I'm afraid we don't have any items to give you to express our gratitude.
But we can give you something else – our word. When your battle with the Demon King comes...
We will come to your aid!

Battle of the Armory.say: The Dämonenhöhle – the Demon King's armory.
Located at the heart of the lava lake, it is a key strategic point for the Demon King Army.
The armory is surrounded by glowing hot lava that has been flowing for untold years. Attacking it is a nightmarish premise.
It is also the base of the Dragonlord's airborne hunters division. They have been incessantly launching attacks on Sentinel Bastion from here.
Today we shall bring an end to it all.
We meet again, Dragonlord! Remember when I kicked your butt?
You ran with your tail between your legs last time, but that's not happening again!
Huh? Are you people serious?
Fine, I admit I got very careless back at your precious wall!
However! Now it's YOU attacking MY base! Do you really think the six of you can take all of us on?
I don't just think – I KNOW we can!
You scared? Then it's not too late to surrender!
Grrrr! You're SO annoying!
Okay, hotshots! You're gonna see what happens when you REALLY mess with the airborne hunters!
Dark Knight Slimejuus, give 'em hell!
CHIIIRP! CHIIIRP!
Big brother! A huge force is coming right at us!
Sounds like the perfect time to use the Seatide Horn.
The horn resounds with a serene note, and the counterattack on the army of monsters begins.
Naturally, our party members aren't the only ones attacking.
With the billow of a saltwater wave, an armada of ghost pirates and their ships join the assault.
With each passing second, their fleet seems to grow larger and the splashing of the waves intensifies.
You sounded the Seatide Horn, so we've come just as promised.
Heehee. It's so nice to see our generous friends again.
Let me guess – you want us to dip our ships in the lava and capture that stronghold, am I right?
Hahahah! Man! I can't remember the last time I fought in such a fun battle!
You can say that again! I've never seen something THIS fun before!
Holy hell... You're the goddess of pioneering and adventure! Royal Fortune!
That's me! Good to see you all again!
I heard someone sound the Seatide Horn way off in the distance.
Thought I'd check it out, and what do I find? You lot, starting the party without me!
So, what are we doing? Fighting the Dragonlord?
Fine by me! Let's take her down!
Aha... Ahaha...
I don't get what's going on!
You have an armada of ghost pirates?
And there's even a goddess on your side?!
This is ridiculous! How was I ever supposed to win?!
The Dragonlord decries the unfairness of it all as she's reduced to bits of mana before vanishing.
Meanwhile, the pirates wipe out all the monsters in and around the lava lake.
Hahaha! Now THAT was a nice battle!
I'm glad I showed up! I've not had this much fun in who knows how long!
Well, Tempestas, you've fulfilled your promise. It's time you come with me.
As the laws of the world dictate, the dead should get to rest in peace.
You heard her, adventurers. We kept our word and have nothing anchoring us to this world anymore.
Finally! I've been dying – literally – for a good rest.
Same... Fighting for hundreds of years takes a serious toll on the body.
Farewell, charitable clients. We'll be praying for your success.
Wait, wait! Before you go, let me snap a picture of you all! Smile for the camera!
Cheese!
Thanks! Goodbye, pirates! Goodbye, goddess!

Battle of the Supply Base.say: After conquering the Demon King's armory and saying our farewells to the pirates, we moved on to our next objective.
We're so close now. This is the final obstacle standing between us and the Demon King's castle – the supply base.
According to the old maps our holy church possesses, this place was once a port city known as Schneehafen.
Some say it was the second-largest settlement in the kingdom before the Demon King seized control...
I can hardly believe I'm here, seeing its ruins with my own eyes.
Big brother, I just finished scouting.
Man, soaring through the skies on Yuni's back was AMAZING!
You should have seen the view from up there. It was unforgettable!
Okay, okay, now shouldn't we report what we saw?
Right. Commander, we found out something crazy!
There aren't any monsters stationed at the supply base! They all ran away!
They abandoned it without a fight? I wonder if seeing what happened to the armory made their morale plummet.
Probably, though I doubt they know the pirates aren't with us anymore.
This is a great opportunity to take advantage of the disarray.
Everyone, move in! Capture the supply base!
Demon King's Supply Base - Lord's Chamber
THUD!
Come on out, archdemon! The hero is here to send you to hell!
Hey... This place is empty! Nobody's here!
So even their leader jumped ship.
There was barely any resistance on the way here, either. I suppose the archdemon cottoned on and legged it.
Did she REALLY run away? I didn't think the Demon King's officers were THIS cowardly.
Big brother... Someone left a letter on the desk.
It could be boobytrapped. Allow me to open it for you.
Dear pesky adventurers: Greetings.
You caused a real mess over at the armory. I would salute you if we were allies.
Clearly, you are absurdly powerful, and it's only a matter of time before you bring down the Demon King's castle.
I, for one, don't feel like sacrificing myself to fight a lost battle.
For that simple reason, I've turned tail and taken my leave.
The supply base is all yours now.
My treasure is in the chest. Take whatever you like.
Should you need any supplies, you'll find them in the depot.
I also left a copy of my personal favorite album behind for you. By all means, give it a listen.
It has recordings of all my live concerts as well as my very best songs.
Yes, I'm just that good of a girl.
Should we ever have the pleasure or displeasure of running into each other again...
Then I ask you to spare my life. Kindly remember that I did you a solid here today!
– Yours truly, Archdemon & Demon King Army Officer & Witch Idol & Good Girl Jade
Wow. She really did abandon the base she was in charge of.
Hmph... At least THIS demon has some sense in her skull.
But this means an archdemon got away... She'll become a problem in the future.
At least she won't get in our way when we're fighting the Demon King herself.
Mhm... She shouldn't.

options.content: I say we let her off the hook.
We'll give her twice the punishment.

Battle of the Supply Base.say: If we ever cross paths with her again...

Angel on a Mission.say: With the supply base conquered, there is nothing left that stands on our path to the Demon King's castle.
So close are we now that you can even clearly see the rising mana from the defensive magic being used within the castle.
The time to finally confront the Demon King is here.
Whoohh... The temperature is getting higher the closer we get.
Nubian, do have any potions that cool you down?
Hmm... I've got some instant freeze potions. Want one?
I said "cool you down," not "turn you into a human popsicle"!
Unicorn! Let me borrow Yuni for a minute!
But I don't wanna...
I just need to fly for a bit and feel the cool wind on my– Hey!
Commander! Up in the sky! Something's coming toward us!
Isn't that... the Fighting Angel?
Dammit! The army must've sent someone to retrieve Jeanne!
Well, I won't let 'em! She's a member of our party now and nobody's taking her on my watch!
Please calm down, everyone. That's not just any soldier in our army – it's Joffre.
I assure you she will understand!
May the goddess of light and justice be with you.
It's good to see you again, adventurers.
What brings you here so suddenly, Joffre?
Her Majesty and the holy church were concerned about you, so they sent me.
As I'm sure you've guessed, Her Majesty became extremely worried when you left without saying a word.
She was also quite shocked to learn that your party had captured the Demon King's bases one after the next.
Consequently, she called upon her troops and ordered the 13th – and hopefully final – major campaign of this millennium.
The bulk of the Royal Army has left Sentinel Bastion and are on their way to aid you in your upcoming battle for the Demon King's castle.
Thank you! I couldn't ask for better news than this! This gives us assurance for the upcoming battle.
Yes, but unfortunately, I have bad news as well.
There exists a mountain range east of the castle. This range has been turned into a den for dragons.
As you know, the dragons have been loyal to the Demon King for centuries. The Great Fire Dragon, their leader, is also one of the Demon King's few hand-picked officers.
If you do not confront the dragons, there's a risk they will come to attack you during your final battle.
A good point. We should definitely deal with the dragons before we take on the Demon King herself.
You certainly should.
Now, I must go. I cannot stay with you any longer as I have to report to Her Majesty and the holy church.
I take it you won't be coming with me, Your Holiness?
No. I am a member of this party, and I will not abandon it now.
Very well. Then may the goddesses watch over you.

Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.say: Before us lies a battleground like something from hell itself.
Centuries or even millennia-old dragons circle the skies above the volcano.
The most eye-catching of them all is a titan of a monster, its body reaching hundreds of meters in length and entirely bathed in hellfire.
That is the Great Fire Dragon, the leader of the dragons and the Demon King's most trusted officer.
Each flap of its wings sends echoes across the mountains and churns the lava below.
Monsters so massive they all but blot out the sky... That's what we're up against.
Do we really have a fighting chance?
They have a crushing numbers advantage, and we're at a severe disadvantage in a head-on clash...
We won't win by ordinary means. It's time to use one of our trump cards.
Let's call in the legendary heroes.
The cool sound of the flute cuts like a knife through the sizzling hot air.
As the final note finishes, four magic portals suddenly appear from thin air.
From each portal emerges one of the legendary figures from long ago.
Nice to see you again, heroes of the new age!
I've never fought anything like that Great Fire Dragon before... This will be a worthwhile battle.
Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.actorName: Pulse of the New Year [Dynamic Kick!]

Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.say: I just got done upgrading my axe! Let's see how well it cuts!
*yaaawn*... I want this over with so I can get back to my nap...
The heroes set foot on the battlefield. Wasting not a moment, they jump straight toward the dragons.
Wait a moment... These girls look familiar...
Aren't they the legendary party that killed the previous Demon King?!
Oh, right. We didn't tell you how we met them, did we?
It was an adventure in and of itself! I'll show you a pic and tell you the story after this battle's over!
ROOOAAARRR!
Its scales are really tough... Dynamic Kick: Two-Hit Combo!
My explosive magic won't take it out in one hit... You'll have to soften it up more.
I'll hit it one more time! Gigaaa Crash!
ROOOAAARRR!
Dammit! It used another monster to block what would've been a crippling hit! Now it's flying away to recover its health!
It's got to be just as strong and intelligent as the Demon King herself... Girls, keep your guard up!
Lemme help you! Hero's Slaaash!
I'm gonna help, too! I've been saving my best potions for a big battle like this!
Here we go! Beast Essence!
While the battle continues on the frontlines, I monitor the situation using my status screen.
In terms of strength, our party does have what it takes to defeat this great dragon.
It won't be so easy, though. Our opponent is clever, using other monsters to throw us off our game while it backs off to recover its health.
The battle has reached a complete stalemate.
Commander, you must let me join the fight!
I can't. You need to stay, or else the clerics will be vulnerable.
Yes, but this battle is going nowhere. The Great Fire Dragon has so much mana it'll slowly wear us down if we don't do something.
It knows exactly what it's doing. This dragon is a clever bastard.
Still, our opponent has made a miscalculation.
A... miscalculation?
Yeah. The dragon didn't account for there being one more person who could influence this battle.

options.content: (Use your Throwing skill against the dragon.)

Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.say: One more person? Wait, you mean...
(I've amassed quite the collection of throwables now.)
(From the bombs I bought back at the city, to the stuff I picked out from the Sentinel Bastion armory, to even a few treasures I picked up on the way.)
(Now it's finally time to bring my arsenal to bear.)
GRAAAGHH?!
A hail storm of explosive items descends upon the dragon. Anyone can see this is far beyond its predictions.
In a fit of panic, it dodges out of the way, missing its chance to heal itself.
And so, the precarious balance in our neck and neck battle tips in our favor. Now we just need to overwhelm them.
Only a few turns later, the Great Fire Dragon has finally been soundly defeated.
Phew... Great work, everyone! That was a magnificent battle.
Yes, to think you had such an awesome skill this whole time... Never judge a book by its cover.
Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.actorName: Pulse of the New Year [Dynamic Kick!]

Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.say: I knew I had to hold on to at least one skill to defend myself, even if I don't personally fight in battles.
Oh, I've been wondering – why doesn't your party have a healer? It can't be easy to adventure without one.
I feel like someone else asked the same thing once...
You don't need a healer if you can defeat all the enemies before they defeat you!
Besides, it's not like our party always consisted of just us four.
We had two healers when we fought today, didn't we?
More is always merrier when your goal is to dethrone the Demon King!
It was good to fight by your side, but now it's time to truly say goodbye.
Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.actorName: Pulse of the New Year [Dynamic Kick!]

Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.say: You and your party have the power. You'll wreck the Demon King, I know it.
Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.actorName: Pulse of the New Year [Dynamic Kick!]

Fighting the Great Fire Dragon.say: Farewell, legendary heroes...
It's sad to see them go. At least now with the dragons out of the way, we can rest for a bit.
Wait! You're forgetting something crucial, Commander!
Am I? What?
The dragon's treasure! We have to loot the den first! No time for breaks!

Showdown with the Demon King.say: The tall and black castle wall is drawing nearer.
Our party has finally reached the end of its journey – the Demon King's castle.
The vampire hunters got here first and have been waiting for us, just as they promised.
There is a third person, too. Someone I wasn't expecting.
Greetings from the goddess of steel and dragons, fearless adventurers.
I heard you were attacking the Demon King's castle, so I came to support you as a representative of my church.
These castle doors are thick. The divine machine I brought will take care of them.
KABOOOM!
The giant steel dragon breathes a furious flame, cutting through the multilayered defensive spells on the gates like a knife through butter.
After the brave and dextrous vampire hunters breach the castle's frontal defenses, they lead us right to the Demon King's throneroom.
Demon King's Castle - Throneroom
Wretched demon! I have come to end you! Oh, and my party's here, too.
Your reign ends today, Demon King!
Tsk... Impudent little insects.
Aren't you that band of adventurers who spied on me back at your precious human castle?
Not only are you still alive, now you've come all the way to my throneroom.
You've been lucky, but your luck ends here. I am the pinnacle of all demonkind – and you're challenging me? You must have a real death wish.
Then I will give you what you desire – the sweet release of death!
If you wanted us dead, you'd have killed us back at the royal castle. Then we would've never gotten this far.
Perhaps so, but even I have standards!
It's my job as the Demon King to wait upon my throne for you heroes to come straight to me.
She's right, you know! That's what they always do!
Bring it on, demon! I've got the perfect potion for the likes of you!
I-I'm ready to provide healing... I'll help as much as I can!
Hmph. Enough talk. Have at you!
Here we go! Our final confrontation with the Demon King is starting!
Feeble adventurers! I curse ye!
Tides of time wash over ye, sap thy strength, and make thee weak! Writhe, writhe!
Kneel! On the ground with all of thee!
*cough cough*... My god... This is the ultimate curse! Only the Demon King can cast it!
We're in serious trouble... This curse is way too advanced for me to lift!
*cough*... Big brother... I'm at my limit...
I can't... even muster the strength to hold a potion...
You fiend... I am the hero! I will not fall at the last step of my journey!
Ahahaha! Can't fight anymore, can you?
I warned you. It was a miracle that you even got this far. Thinking you could defeat me was utter foolishness.
Now you will die a slow and painful death from my curse!
"Notification: Your skills Curse Resistance SSS and Blessing of Light SSS are in effect."
Hm? How in the world are you still standing?
No matter. My curse has still left the rest of your entire party squirming on the floor.
Even if your abilities let you resist my curse, you're powerless to do anything against me.
"Powerless," you say?
You might want to take a look at my stats. You have the power to do that, don't you?
Hah! Of course I do. I am the apex of demonkind – seeing an adventurer's stats is child's play for...
What... the hell?!
No... That's impossible...
Took you long enough.
How does one even raise their base stats that ludicrously high?
You're telling me it WASN'T pure luck that you killed my Great Fire Dragon?!
Damn right. I can even beat you all by myself.
You little rat! You kept your stats to yourself this whole time!
Out of your whole party, YOU'RE the strongest by a long shot!

options.content: (Pick up Fu Shun's sword with your one hand.)
(Pick up Jeanne d'Arc's sword with the other.)

Showdown with the Demon King.say: Wh-what are you doing?
Wait! We can still talk this out!
I'm the Demon King, remember? Whatever your king promised you for my head, I'll make it double!
NO! GET AWAY FROM MEEE!

sequence: Thus, the Demon King was slain.
The world was at peace once more.

The Story Thereafter.say: The party of heroes returned to the Royal Capital and were given a warm welcome befitting their valorous feat.
The jubilant people threw a grand celebration that lasted over half a month.
Though the saint Jeanne d'Arc did flee her post, she was not punished for it.
After all the festivities, Fu Shun suggested that the party pay a visit to Ursp Village.
There, the village mayor hosted a spectacular banquet for the triumphant heroes as she had promised.
While more modest than the celebration in the capital, it was certainly more intimate and full of warmth.
Following that, Nubian returned to Thermalq Village and went back to providing free medical care for the villagers.
Now famous for her actions, this potion maker experienced an endless influx of nobles coming all the way from the capital to seek her services.
One day, she acquired herself a grand mansion on the outskirts of the village.
The potion maker never did bring anyone else to Silverbank Island. She was simply too busy with other things.
As such, the wise and mysterious witch Arkhangelsk ended up forgotten by history.
Liverpool and Unicorn joined the holy church in the capital and devoted themselves to honing their skills.
Later, they went on to become the right-hand women of the new pontiff, Jeanne d'Arc.
Their service continued for the rest of their long lives granted by their goddess.
After committing hundreds of years of their lives to fighting a lonesome war in the uninhabited wastes, the vampire hunters were finally acknowledged and accepted by the kingdom.
Sheffield and Seydlitz both were given noble titles and the honor of ruling the enormous territory in and around the central fortress that the vampires once held.
Word that life had returned to the Wasteland quickly reached all the corners of the kingdom.
As a result, the whole kingdom experienced a settlement boom.
Fei Yuen, the mayor of Friede Village, rode this wave of expansion and led a group of settlers to reclaim the ruins of the port city Schneehafen.
The lands were purified, bringing back the snowfall. Water once more filled the Frozen Lake rather than lava, and the former ruins were reborn into a great city full of life.
The one who led the settlers to the city, Fei Yuen, would later become its mayor.
The Demon King's defeat led to the kingdom becoming quite busy.
Having seen the destruction of the Demon King's castle, the Royal Army split into groups and left Sentinel Bastion behind.
Each of the loyal knights led their own battalion, establishing countless new bases across the revitalized lands, watching over settlers and travelers alike.
The restoration of the Royal Crown Wall Ruins went orderly, thanks to the oversight of the holy church.
The ruins of the old Royal Capital, on the other hand, were simply too decayed. The king was forced to give up on her plans to restore them.
However, the many artifacts buried among the rubble proved a great source of wealth all the same.
To this end, the army issued a large number of commissions to guilds, attracting countless adventurers to explore the ruins.
Among them, Fu Shun the Hero.
She was offered to join the Royal Knights, but declined, instead choosing to continue traveling the Wasteland.
She knew that somewhere out there, an archdemon was waiting for an opportunity to rise from the darkness.
Finally, there's the fate of that commander, which remains a mystery.
Some say this hero became a lord over some remote region.
Others say this legendary figure went back to the witch in the tower and became her disciple for a hundred years.
There are also some local chronicles that say the commander created a new party and traveled to distant lands in search of the New World.
Some folk records even claim that this human was in fact the true Demon King and was now in control of the entire kingdom.
But, if you asked the other members of this legendary party what they thought...
They would tell you that the commander is probably off in some peaceful place, enjoying the quiet life.

sequence: But for now, you can put down your swords and rest.

Epilogue.say: After closing the game, I head to the Iron Blood dorm to speak to Friedrich Carl.
*knock knock*
Come in! The door is unlocked.
Stepping inside her room, I see her lying down on the carpet.
Heehee. If it isn't the brave little soul who dethroned the Demon King. Why are you here?
